title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: build six consortia bundled misosoup24 dataset use throughout vignette.
code: data("misosoup24") cm_list <- lapply(seq_len(6), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i]     ) })  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(cm_list, name = "Demo")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: align(CM, CM) compares two ConsortiumMetabolism objects returns CMA Type = "pairwise".
code: cma <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]]) cma #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment #> Name: "ac_A1R12_1 vs ac_A1R12_10" #> Type: "pairwise" #> Metric: "FOS" #> Score: 0.7634 #> Query: "ac_A1R12_1", Reference: "ac_A1R12_10" #> Coverage: query 0.763, reference 0.38 #> Pathways: 71 shared, 22 query-only, 116 reference-only.

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Five metrics available via method argument. Regardless selected primary score, applicable metrics always computed stored. Metabolic Alignment Aggregate Score (MAAS) combines four metrics. Weights renormalised metrics unavailable (e.g., unweighted networks lack Bray-Curtis):
code: cma_fos <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]], method = "FOS") cma_jac <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]], method = "jaccard") scores(cma_fos) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $coverageQuery #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageReference #> [1] 0.3796791 cma_maas <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]], method = "MAAS") scores(cma_maas) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $coverageQuery #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageReference #> [1] 0.3796791 #>  #> $MAAS #> [1] 0.5035647

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: FOS uses Szymkiewicz-Simpson coefficient, divides smaller network. means FOS = 1 whenever small consortium strict functional subset larger one – even larger consortium many additional pathways. detect , ramen reports coverage ratios alongside similarity metrics: coverageQuery: fraction query’s pathways found reference coverageReference: fraction reference’s pathways found query FOS high one coverage ratio low, alignment represents subset relationship rather true functional equivalence. symmetric similarity, use Jaccard instead.
code: scores(cma_fos)[c("FOS", "coverageQuery", "coverageReference")] #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageQuery #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageReference #> [1] 0.3796791

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: alignment classifies every metabolite--metabolite pathway shared, unique query, unique reference.
code: ## All pathways in the alignment head(pathways(cma)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   consumed produced #>   <chr>    <chr>    #> 1 acald    ac       #> 2 asp__L   ac       #> 3 gly      ac       #> 4 gthrd    ac       #> 5 h2o2     ac       #> 6 h2s      ac  ## Shared pathways only shared <- pathways(cma, type = "shared") nrow(shared) #> [1] 71  ## Unique pathways (returns a list with $query and $reference) unique_pw <- pathways(cma, type = "unique") nrow(unique_pw$query) #> [1] 22 nrow(unique_pw$reference) #> [1] 116

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Statistical significance assessed degree-preserving network rewiring. query network’s pathways shuffled preserving metabolite’s degree, metric recomputed null distribution. MAAS, binary network topology permuted; weighted assays (Consumption, Production, nSpecies) remain fixed, null distribution reflects topological variation composite score.
code: cma_p <- align(     cm_list[[1]],     cm_list[[2]],     method = "FOS",     computePvalue = TRUE,     nPermutations = 99L ) scores(cma_p) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $coverageQuery #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageReference #> [1] 0.3796791 #>  #> $pvalue #> [1] 0.01

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: hold first consortium query search database built remaining five: top-hit name score sit ReferenceName / PrimaryScore. full ranked table stored Scores$ranking: row holds reference consortium’s name, primary score (requested method), four individual metric columns, coverage ratios query. Rows pre-sorted score descending order.
code: query <- cm_list[[1]] db <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(cm_list[-1], name = "db") hits <- align(query, db) #> Searching 5 consortia using "FOS". hits #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment  #> Name: "ac_A1R12_1 vs CMS (5 consortia)" #> Type: "search" #> Metric: "FOS" #> Score: 0.7849 #> Query: "ac_A1R12_1", Top hit: "ac_A1R12_12" (of 5 consortia) ranking <- scores(hits)$ranking head(ranking) #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   reference   score   FOS jaccard brayCurtis redundancyOverlap coverageQuery #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 ac_A1R12_12 0.785 0.785   0.451      0.635             0.451         0.785 #> 2 ac_A1R12_14 0.785 0.785   0.497      0.553             0.497         0.785 #> 3 ac_A1R12_10 0.763 0.763   0.340      0.312             0.340         0.763 #> 4 ac_A1R12_11 0.538 0.538   0.226      0.500             0.226         0.538 #> 5 ac_A1R12_13 0.538 0.538   0.270      0.515             0.270         0.538 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: coverageReference <dbl>

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Use topK truncate ranked table – useful database large best matches matter: similarityMatrix() returns 1 x n row vector (1 x topK truncated), query name row label: Pathway correspondences always reflect single overall top hit, regardless topK:
code: hits_top3 <- align(query, db, topK = 3L) #> Searching 5 consortia using "FOS". scores(hits_top3)$ranking #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   reference   score   FOS jaccard brayCurtis redundancyOverlap coverageQuery #>   <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 ac_A1R12_12 0.785 0.785   0.451      0.635             0.451         0.785 #> 2 ac_A1R12_14 0.785 0.785   0.497      0.553             0.497         0.785 #> 3 ac_A1R12_10 0.763 0.763   0.340      0.312             0.340         0.763 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: coverageReference <dbl> round(similarityMatrix(hits_top3), 3) #>            ac_A1R12_12 ac_A1R12_14 ac_A1R12_10 #> ac_A1R12_1       0.785       0.785       0.763 head(pathways(hits_top3, type = "shared")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   consumed produced #>   <chr>    <chr>    #> 1 asp__L   ac       #> 2 etoh     ac       #> 3 gly      ac       #> 4 gthrd    ac       #> 5 h2o2     ac       #> 6 pyr      ac

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen (Reconstruction Alignment Microbial Exchange Networks) analyses functional similarity microbial communities metabolic network perspective. Rather comparing communities species composition, ramen asks: metabolite--metabolite pathways community catalyse, similar resulting networks?

title: Introduction to ramen
text: Throughout ramen, pathway directed edge one metabolite another: “metabolite consumed metabolite B produced least one species.” biochemical pathway KEGG MetaCyc sense (e.g. glycolysis). Rather, captures single metabolic exchange coupling within community. consortium’s full set pathways forms square metabolite--metabolite network encodes collective metabolic capability.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: package provides three S4 classes build Bioconductor’s TreeSummarizedExperiment: ConsortiumMetabolism (CM) – single community’s metabolic exchange network ConsortiumMetabolismSet (CMS) – collection CMs precomputed overlap scores dendrogram ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment (CMA) – result aligning two communities vignette walks complete analysis data import alignment using bundled misosoup24 dataset (56 metabolic solutions MiSoSoup). details alignment metrics, see vignette("alignment", package = "ramen"); gallery plot types, see vignette("visualisation", package = "ramen").

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen deliberately uses term consortium throughout. consortium defined metabolic function – set metabolite--metabolite pathways member species collectively catalyse – taxonomic composition. Two assemblages entirely different species rosters identical exchange networks , ramen’s perspective, consortium; conversely, two assemblages species different active pathways different consortia. functional framing follows long-standing argument microbial ecology ecosystem behaviour robustly predicted microbes microbes present (Falkowski, Fenchel & Delong 2008, Science 320:1034).

title: Introduction to ramen
text: short glossary terms used throughout package: Consortium – microbial assemblage characterised metabolic exchange network, species composition (see ). Pathway – ramen sense, directed metabolite--metabolite edge: metabolite consumed metabolite B produced least one species. KEGG/MetaCyc biochemical pathway. Assay – one six m x m metabolite--metabolite matrices stored ConsortiumMetabolism object (Binary, nSpecies, Consumption, Production, EffectiveConsumption, EffectiveProduction). FOS – Functional Overlap Score; primary similarity metric two consortia, computed Szymkiewicz-Simpson coefficient expanded binary pathway matrices. Szymkiewicz-Simpson – asymmetric set-overlap coefficient |X intersect Y| / min(|X|, |Y|) measures completely smaller set contained larger. Alignment – operation putting two consortia correspondence shared metabolite/pathway space summarising similarity, returning ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment. MAAS – Metabolite Abundance Adjusted Score; flux-magnitude-weighted variant FOS -weights pathways small fluxes.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen ships misosoup24, list 56 metabolic solutions. element data.frame columns metabolites, species, fluxes, negative fluxes indicate consumption positive fluxes indicate production. Metabolite identifiers follow BiGG namespace (King et al. 2016): short lowercase codes ac (acetate), co2 (CO2), etoh (ethanol), pyr (pyruvate). Double-underscores encode stereochemistry: ala__L = L-alanine, ala__D = D-alanine. Look identifier https://bigg.ucsd.edu/metabolites.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen performs string-equality matching metabolite identifiers. Two species consume molecule different naming conventions appear consume two distinct metabolites network: CM whose edge list mixes ac_e (BiGG), CHEBI:30089 (ChEBI), acetate (free text) compound yield three separate metabolite nodes rather one. applies across CMs ConsortiumMetabolismSet across operands align() call: identifiers must match character--character treated metabolite. Pick one namespace stick across CMs CMS alignment. package currently provide name-normalisation helper; cross-namespace mapping (BiGG ChEBI, ChEBI free text, etc.) user’s responsibility, ideally performed data-import step constructing CMs.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: string-equality contract applies species names. consortium labels strain E_coli another labels strain E.coli, Ecoli, Escherichia_coli treated harbouring three four distinct species CMs assembled ConsortiumMetabolismSet, compared via compareSpecies(), aligned align(). mismatch silent: warning, just inflated species count spurious zeros cross-consortium overlap. Common typo patterns quietly fragment single organism several case differences (E_coli vs e_coli vs E_Coli), separator differences (E_coli vs E.coli vs E-coli vs E coli), abbreviation versus full name (Ecoli vs E.coli vs Escherichia_coli vs Escherichia coli), inconsistent strain suffixes (E.coli_K12 vs E.coli_k12 vs bare E.coli). minimal first pass collapses case separators, catches first two classes: Abbreviation--full-name reconciliation strain-suffix disambiguation genuinely harder typically require project-specific lookup table; pick canonical scheme, normalise data-import step, apply constructing CM. ramen currently ship cleanSpecies() helper, hygiene step user’s responsibility – caveat applies symmetrically every downstream comparison, including compareSpecies() cross-consortium align(). Caution – alternative optima. MiSoSoup Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) enumerator: single underlying metabolic model returns multiple alternative optimal solutions growth problem. Two alternatives typically share pathways produce high pairwise FOS values. high overlap expected construction reflects solver consistency, biological similarity. interpreting overlap scores, compare consortia derived different models conditions; treat overlap alternatives model sanity check enumeration, ecological signal.
code: data$species <- gsub("[._\\s-]+", "_", tolower(data$species)) data("misosoup24") length(misosoup24) #> [1] 56 names(misosoup24)[1:8] #> [1] "ac_A1R12_1"  "ac_A1R12_10" "ac_A1R12_11" "ac_A1R12_12" "ac_A1R12_13" #> [6] "ac_A1R12_14" "ac_A1R12_15" "ac_A1R12_16" head(misosoup24[[1]]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   metabolite species    flux #>   <chr>      <chr>     <dbl> #> 1 ac         A1R12     0.773 #> 2 ac         I2R16   -10.8   #> 3 acald      A1R12    -1.12  #> 4 acald      I2R16     1.12  #> 5 ala__D     A1R12     0.760 #> 6 ala__D     I2R16    -0.760

title: Introduction to ramen
text: raw MiSoSoup output (nested YAML), importMisosoup() parses data directly ConsortiumMetabolismSet containing one ConsortiumMetabolism per viable solution. function accepts single YAML file, directory YAML files, pre-loaded nested list yaml::read_yaml(). Media-level exchange bounds stashed CM’s metadata() slot $media remain accessible needed.
code: ## Single file -> CMS (not run -- requires external data) cms <- importMisosoup("path/to/misosoup_output.yaml")  ## Directory of YAML files -> CMS merging all consortia cms <- importMisosoup("path/to/misosoup_dir/", name = "experiment1")  ## Pre-loaded list -> CMS (name required) raw <- yaml::read_yaml("path/to/misosoup_output.yaml") cms <- importMisosoup(raw, name = "experiment1")  ## Access stashed media bounds for a specific CM ## S4Vectors::metadata(cms@Consortia[[1]])$media

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ConsortiumMetabolism() constructor accepts data.frame columns species, metabolite, flux. Custom column names can specified via species_col, metabolite_col, flux_col. data wide format separate columns consumed produced metabolites, pivotCM() converts long format ConsortiumMetabolism() expects:
code: wide_data <- data.frame(     species = c("Sp_A", "Sp_B", "Sp_C"),     uptake = c("met1", "met2", "met3"),     secretion = c("met2", "met3", "met1"),     flux = c(1, 1, 1) )  long_data <- pivotCM(     wide_data,     species = "species",     from = "uptake",     to = "secretion",     flux = "flux" ) head(long_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   species met    flux #>   <chr>   <chr> <dbl> #> 1 Sp_A    met1     -1 #> 2 Sp_A    met2      1 #> 3 Sp_B    met2     -1 #> 4 Sp_B    met3      1 #> 5 Sp_C    met3     -1 #> 6 Sp_C    met1      1

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ConsortiumMetabolism() accepts long-format data.frame columns species, metabolite, flux (negative = consumption, positive = production). makes interoperation Python-based MICOM cobrapy ecosystems straightforward: export per-species exchange fluxes CSV Python, read R. minimal MICOM growth solution can exported follows: pattern works plain cobra.Model: iterate model.exchanges, record carrying species, write columns . R side:
code: import pandas as pd from micom import Community from micom.workflows import grow  ## com is a pre-built micom.Community sol = com.cooperative_tradeoff()  ## sol.exchanges is a tidy DataFrame with columns ##   reaction, metabolite, taxon (species), flux, ... exchanges = sol.exchanges[["taxon", "metabolite", "flux"]] exchanges.columns = ["species", "metabolite", "flux"] exchanges.to_csv("micom_exchanges.csv", index = False) exch <- read.csv("micom_exchanges.csv") cm_micom <- ConsortiumMetabolism(exch, name = "micom_run1")

title: Introduction to ramen
code: cm1 <- ConsortiumMetabolism(     misosoup24[[1]],     name = names(misosoup24)[1] ) cm1 #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "ac_A1R12_1" #> Weighted metabolic network: 2 species, 14 metabolites, 93 pathways. #> Pathways per species: min 45, mean 46.5, max 48.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: quick testing, synCM() generates random consortium:
code: cm_syn <- synCM("Synthetic", n_species = 4, max_met = 8, seed = 42) cm_syn #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "Synthetic" #> Weighted metabolic network: 4 species, 8 metabolites, 13 pathways. #> Pathways per species: min 1, mean 4, max 12.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: CM inherits TreeSummarizedExperiment, standard dimension accessors work. Rows columns correspond metabolites (square metabolite--metabolite matrix): six assay matrices encode different views network:
code: dim(cm1) #> [1] 14 14 SummarizedExperiment::assayNames(cm1) #> [1] "Binary"                       "nSpecies"                     #> [3] "Consumption"                  "Production"                   #> [5] "EffectiveConsumption"         "EffectiveProduction"          #> [7] "nEffectiveSpeciesConsumption" "nEffectiveSpeciesProduction"

title: Introduction to ramen
text: pathways() returns per-pathway summary statistics: .data.frame() returns underlying edge list. Use export CM’s data downstream tools: (consortia() reserved containers CMs – ConsortiumMetabolismSet ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment – since noun plural.) Replacement methods allow renaming:
code: name(cm1) #> [1] "ac_A1R12_1" species(cm1) #> [1] "A1R12" "I2R16" metabolites(cm1) #>  [1] "ac"     "acald"  "ala__D" "ala__L" "asp__L" "co2"    "etoh"   "glu__L" #>  [9] "gly"    "gthox"  "gthrd"  "h2o2"   "h2s"    "pyr" head(pathways(cm1)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   consumed produced n_species #>   <chr>    <chr>        <dbl> #> 1 ac       acald            1 #> 2 ac       asp__L           1 #> 3 ac       co2              1 #> 4 ac       etoh             1 #> 5 ac       gly              1 #> 6 ac       gthrd            1 head(as.data.frame(cm1)) #>      met species        flux #> 1     ac   A1R12   0.7729250 #> 2     ac   I2R16 -10.7673345 #> 3  acald   A1R12  -1.1209639 #> 4  acald   I2R16   1.1209639 #> 5 ala__D   A1R12   0.7603136 #> 6 ala__D   I2R16  -0.7603136 name(cm1) <- "first_solution" name(cm1) #> [1] "first_solution"

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ConsortiumMetabolismSet groups multiple CMs. construction, ramen automatically expands binary matrices shared universal metabolite space, computes pairwise overlap scores, builds hierarchical clustering dendrogram. default constructor prints step--step progress trace; pass verbose = FALSE silence .
code: ## Build 20 CMs from misosoup24 cm_list <- lapply(seq_len(20), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i]     ) })  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(     cm_list,     name = "MiSoSoup_20",     verbose = FALSE ) cms

title: Introduction to ramen
text: species() returns species number distinct pathways catalyses: Filter metabolic role – generalists (top fraction pathway count) specialists (bottom fraction):

title: Introduction to ramen
text: pathways() type argument classifies pathways prevalence: quantileCutoff parameter controls threshold (default 0.1).
code: ## Pan-consortia: present in many consortia (top fraction) head(pathways(cms, type = "pan-cons")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   consumed produced n_species n_cons #>   <chr>    <chr>        <int>  <int> #> 1 ac       pyr             20     20 #> 2 ala__L   pyr             20     20 #> 3 etoh     co2              5     20 #> 4 h2s      co2             15     20 #> 5 pyr      ac               1     20 #> 6 pyr      ala__L           1     20  ## Niche: present in few consortia (bottom fraction) head(pathways(cms, type = "niche")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   consumed     produced n_species n_cons #>   <chr>        <chr>        <int>  <int> #> 1 LalaDgluMdap ac               1      1 #> 2 LalaDgluMdap ala__D           1      1 #> 3 LalaDgluMdap ala__L           1      1 #> 4 LalaDgluMdap co2              1      1 #> 5 LalaDgluMdap etoh             1      1 #> 6 LalaDgluMdap gthox            1      1

title: Introduction to ramen
text: functionalGroups() clusters species Jaccard similarity pathway sets. plotFunctionalGroups() visualises resulting dendrogram: Functional groups dendrogram.
code: fg <- functionalGroups(cms) plotFunctionalGroups(fg, k = 3)

title: Introduction to ramen
text: CMS dendrogram numbered nodes. numbered internal nodes can used extract sub-clusters:
code: plot(cms) sub_cms <- extractCluster(cms, node_id = 1) sub_cms

title: Introduction to ramen
text: align() compares two CMs returns ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment: similarity metrics computed automatically: Pathway correspondences show shared unique pathways:
code: cma_pair <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]]) cma_pair #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment #> Name: "ac_A1R12_1 vs ac_A1R12_10" #> Type: "pairwise" #> Metric: "FOS" #> Score: 0.7634 #> Query: "ac_A1R12_1", Reference: "ac_A1R12_10" #> Coverage: query 0.763, reference 0.38 #> Pathways: 71 shared, 22 query-only, 116 reference-only. scores(cma_pair) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $coverageQuery #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageReference #> [1] 0.3796791 shared <- pathways(cma_pair, type = "shared") unique_pw <- pathways(cma_pair, type = "unique") nrow(shared) #> [1] 71 nrow(unique_pw$query) #> [1] 22 nrow(unique_pw$reference) #> [1] 116

title: Introduction to ramen

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen classes inherit TreeSummarizedExperiment, standard SummarizedExperiment accessors work box. ramen first-class Bioconductor citizen, isolated framework – can mix objects freely SE-based tools. Pull raw assay matrix: rows columns CM metabolites (square m x m network), dim(), rowData(), colData() act metabolite space: ConsortiumMetabolismSet likewise exposes SE interface, columns indexing universal metabolite space: Subsetting works metabolite space using standard [, j] syntax – , first 5 rows 5 columns: means downstream Bioconductor tooling – e.g. mia microbiome analysis package consuming (Tree)SummarizedExperiment objects – can operate ramen outputs without conversion.

title: Visualisation
text: ramen provides plot() methods three core classes: ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment. vignette visual gallery every plot type. context underlying analyses, see vignette("ramen", package = "ramen") vignette("alignment", package = "ramen"). ramen plot methods return ggplot objects, composable usual ggplot2 operators (e.g. + ggplot2::theme_minimal()). Network plots rendered ggraph package.
code: library(ramen)

title: Visualisation
code: data("misosoup24") cm_list <- lapply(seq_len(6), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i]     ) })  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(cm_list, name = "Demo") cma_pair <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]]) cma_mult <- align(cms)

title: Visualisation
text: plot(CM) renders directed metabolic flow network using ggraph. Nodes coloured role: lightblue sources (outgoing edges), salmon sinks (incoming edges), lightgoldenrod intermediate nodes (incoming outgoing). type argument selects assay matrix determines edge weights.

title: Visualisation
text: default view shows network structure uniform edge weights. Binary metabolic network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Binary")

title: Visualisation
text: Edge weights reflect many species catalyse pathway. nSpecies-weighted network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "nSpecies")

title: Visualisation
text: Edges weighted summed flux magnitudes. Consumption-weighted network. Production-weighted network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Consumption") plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Production")

title: Visualisation
text: Effective diversity measures evenly species contribute pathway (Shannon-based effective number species). Effective consumption network. Effective production network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "EffectiveConsumption") plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "EffectiveProduction")

title: Visualisation
text: plot(CMS) draws hierarchical clustering dendrogram numbered internal nodes. node IDs used extractCluster() pull sub-clusters. CMS dendrogram numbered nodes.
code: plot(cms)

title: Visualisation
text: Supply data.frame mapping leaf labels colours. CMS dendrogram coloured labels.
code: colour_map <- data.frame(     label = names(misosoup24)[seq_len(6)],     colour = c(         "steelblue", "firebrick", "forestgreen",         "darkorange", "purple", "darkred"     ) ) plot(cms, label_colours = colour_map)

title: Visualisation
text: CMA plot() method supports three types: "heatmap", "network", "scores". default "network" pairwise "heatmap" multiple alignments.

title: Visualisation
text: Pairwise FOS similarities dendrogram-based ordering. Values range 0 (overlap) 1 (identical). Similarity heatmap.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "heatmap")

title: Visualisation
text: Shared, query-unique, reference-unique pathways coloured using colour-blind-safe Okabe-Ito palette (bluish-green, blue, vermilion). legend titled Pathway type. Pairwise alignment network.
code: plot(cma_pair, type = "network")

title: Visualisation
text: bar chart displays similarity metrics pairwise alignments summary statistics multiple alignments. Pairwise alignment scores. Multiple alignment summary scores.
code: plot(cma_pair, type = "scores") plot(cma_mult, type = "scores")

text: fine-grained control network layout, call plotDirectedFlow() directly igraph object. function underlying CM CMA network plots. Custom directed flow plot.

title: Reconstruction and Alignment of Microbial Exchange Networks
text: ramen (Reconstruction Alignment Microbial Exchange Networks) compares microbial communities metabolic exchange networks rather species composition. Given species-metabolite-flux data (e.g. community flux balance analysis), ramen reconstructs directed metabolite--metabolite networks quantifies functionally similar different communities – even share species .

title: Reconstruction and Alignment of Microbial Exchange Networks
text: full workflow – data import alignment, functional groups, visualization – see vignette("ramen", package = "ramen").
code: library(ramen) data("misosoup24")  ## Build two consortium objects cm1 <- ConsortiumMetabolism(     misosoup24[[1]], name = names(misosoup24)[1] ) cm2 <- ConsortiumMetabolism(     misosoup24[[2]], name = names(misosoup24)[2] )  ## Align them cma <- align(cm1, cm2) scores(cma) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $coverageQuery #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $coverageReference #> [1] 0.3796791

title: Reconstruction and Alignment of Microbial Exchange Networks
text: Bundle handful consortia ConsortiumMetabolismSet, align --, plot resulting similarity matrix:
code: sel <- names(misosoup24)[seq_len(8)] cms <- do.call(     ConsortiumMetabolismSet,     c(         lapply(sel, function(n) {             ConsortiumMetabolism(misosoup24[[n]], name = n)         }),         list(name = "misosoup24[1:8]", verbose = FALSE)     ) ) plot(align(cms))

title: Reconstruction and Alignment of Microbial Exchange Networks
text: Import species-metabolite-flux data (MiSoSoup YAML, CSV, data.frame) Build ConsortiumMetabolism objects (one per community) Group ConsortiumMetabolismSet (computes pairwise overlap clustering) Align communities pairwise across full set using five complementary metrics (FOS, Jaccard, Bray-Curtis, Redundancy Overlap, MAAS) Visualise heatmaps, network plots, dendrograms, functional group analyses three classes inherit Bioconductor’s TreeSummarizedExperiment, standard accessor functions (assay(), colData(), dim(), etc.) work box.

title: Reconstruction and Alignment of Microbial Exchange Networks
text: vignette("ramen") – full introduction analysis pipeline vignette("alignment") – alignment metrics, coverage ratios, permutation p-values vignette("visualisation") – gallery plot types

title: Align Consortium Metabolisms — align
text: x ConsortiumMetabolism object (query). y ConsortiumMetabolismSet object (database). method Character scalar specifying primary metric used rank hits. One "FOS" (default), "jaccard", "brayCurtis", "redundancyOverlap", "MAAS". ... Additional arguments (currently unused). computePvalue Logical; compute permutation p-value top hit? Default FALSE. supported "brayCurtis" "redundancyOverlap". nPermutations Integer; number permutations used computePvalue = TRUE. Default 999L. linkage Character scalar specifying agglomeration method hierarchical clustering. Passed hclust. One "complete" (default), "average", "single", "ward.D2". BPPARAM BiocParallel::BiocParallelParam object. Default BiocParallel::SerialParam(). metrics Character vector metrics compute per hit. Defaults four base metrics ("FOS", "jaccard", "brayCurtis", "redundancyOverlap"). Restrict subset (e.g. metrics = "FOS") skip weighted-assay expansion speed large-database searches. "MAAS" primary metric requires four components. topK Integer; non-NULL, truncate ranked results (SimilarityMatrix) top topK hits. overall top hit's pathway correspondences always reported regardless topK. Default NULL (hits).

title: Import MiSoSoup YAML Output — importMisosoup
text: Imports raw MiSoSoup YAML output ConsortiumMetabolismSet object. MiSoSoup YAMLs describe many consortia per file (one per substrate / second-level-key / solution triple), single call always produces CMS regardless whether input single file, directory files, pre-loaded nested list.

title: Import MiSoSoup YAML Output — importMisosoup
code: importMisosoup(   data,   name = NULL,   normalize_ids = TRUE,   biomassPattern = "(?i)^.*?(?:^|_)(?:biomass|growth)_",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Import MiSoSoup YAML Output — importMisosoup
text: data Either file path single MiSoSoup YAML, directory path containing multiple YAML files, pre-loaded nested list yaml::read_yaml(). name Name returned ConsortiumMetabolismSet. NULL (default), name derived input filename (file) directory basename (directory). Required data pre-loaded list. normalize_ids TRUE (default), normalize metabolite IDs via .normalizeBiggIds() (strip R_EX_ prefix, _e suffix) decode COBRApy __NN__ escape sequences via .decodeBiggEscapes(). FALSE, keep metabolite IDs raw form. biomassPattern Perl-compatible regex identifying biomass reaction rows. pattern must match consume entire prefix start reaction ID separator species ID, used detection species extraction (via sub()). Defaults "(?)^.*?(?:^|_)(?:biomass|growth)_", covers three observed MiSoSoup variants (Growth_<sp>, R_Biomass_<sp>, R_R_BIOMASS_<sp>) case-insensitively correctly rejects community_growth summary row. Override MiSoSoup output uses non-standard biomass reaction name, e.g. biomassPattern = "(?)^.*?MYBIO_". verbose TRUE (default), emit progress messages via cli::cli_inform() show progress bar importing directory.

title: Import MiSoSoup YAML Output — importMisosoup
text: ConsortiumMetabolismSet object containing one ConsortiumMetabolism per viable consortium found input. Zero-growth solutions silently skipped.

title: Import MiSoSoup YAML Output — importMisosoup
text: function auto-detects format differences MiSoSoup runs: biomass-reaction name (via configurable regex defaults matching Growth_<sp>, R_Biomass_<sp>, R_R_BIOMASS_<sp> case-insensitively — MiSoSoup derives name underlying model, varies), COBRApy-style __NN__ escape sequences reaction IDs (e.g. __40__ (), second-level key substrate. second-level key either min — indicating CMSC (complete minimal supplying community: viable focal strain single member grows alone) — focal-strain ID, indicating MSC (minimal supplying community strain). Per-consortium metadata set returned CM: metadata(cm)$media — media-level exchange fluxes (reactions without species suffix), preserving original bounds. metadata(cm)$communityGrowth — scalar community_growth summary row MiSoSoup solution, NA_real_ absent. metadata(cm)$misosoupMode — "CMSC" "MSC". metadata(cm)$focalStrain — focal-strain ID (character) MSC consortia, NA_character_ CMSC.

title: Import MiSoSoup YAML Output — importMisosoup
code: # Build a minimal in-memory MiSoSoup-shaped list and import it. In # practice you would point at a real YAML file or a directory of # YAMLs from a MiSoSoup run. raw <- list(     glucose = list(         min = list(             list(                 community = list(y_sp1 = 1, y_sp2 = 1),                 solution = list(                     R_Biomass_sp1 = 0.4,                     R_Biomass_sp2 = 0.3,                     R_EX_glc__D_e_sp1_i = -10,                     R_EX_ac_e_sp1_i = 5,                     R_EX_ac_e_sp2_i = -4,                     R_EX_co2_e_sp2_i = 3,                     community_growth = 0.7                 )             )         )     ) ) cms <- importMisosoup(raw, name = "demo", verbose = FALSE) #>  #> ── Creating CMS "demo" ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Validating 1 <ConsortiumMetabolism> object #> ✔ Validating 1 <ConsortiumMetabolism> object [11ms] #>  #> ℹ Collecting metabolites from 1 consortia #> ✔ Collecting metabolites from 1 consortia [27ms] #>  #> ℹ Re-indexing 3 unique metabolites #> ✔ Re-indexing 3 unique metabolites [26ms] #>  #> ℹ Expanding 1 binary matrices to 3-dimensional space #> ✔ Expanding 1 binary matrices to 3-dimensional space [22ms] #>  #> ℹ Computing 3 x 3 levels matrix #> ✔ Computing 3 x 3 levels matrix [31ms] #>  #> ℹ Computing pairwise overlap (0 pairs via crossprod) #> ✔ Computing pairwise overlap (0 pairs via crossprod) [20ms] #>  #> ℹ Assembling pathway data from 1 consortia #> ✔ Assembling pathway data from 1 consortia [28ms] #>  #> ℹ Collecting 1 consortium graphs #> CMS "demo" created: 1 consortia, 3 metabolites (0.2s) #> ✔ Collecting 1 consortium graphs [76ms] #>  cms #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismSet  #> Name: "demo" #> 1 consortia, 2 species, 3 metabolites. #> Community size (species): min 2, mean 2, max 2. #> Community size (metabolites): min 3, mean 3, max 3.  # Real-world usage (commented; needs an actual MiSoSoup YAML on disk): # cms <- importMisosoup("path/to/misosoup.yaml") # cms <- importMisosoup("path/to/misosoup_dir/", name = "experiment1")

title: Example Data from MiSoSoup 2024 — misosoup24
text: named list 56 data.frames, columns: species Character, species identifier. species flagged focal appears list element name (see naming convention Details); remaining species support community selected MILP solver keep focal strain viable. metabolite Character, metabolite identifier following BiGG namespace (King et al. 2016). Common examples: ac (acetate), co2 (CO2), etoh (ethanol), pyr (pyruvate), ala__L (L-alanine), ala__D (D-alanine). double-underscore convention encodes stereochemistry (e.g. __L = L-isomer, __D = D-isomer). See https://bigg.ucsd.edu full namespace. flux Numeric, metabolic flux. Negative values denote consumption (uptake species), positive values denote production (secretion).

title: Example Data from MiSoSoup 2024 — misosoup24
text: named list 56 data.frames.

title: Example Data from MiSoSoup 2024 — misosoup24
text: MiSoSoup . MiSoSoup Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) enumerator , given community-level metabolic-modelling objective, returns multiple alternative optimal community compositions satisfying objective. output ramen consumes per-solution exchange-flux table: species exchange metabolites, flux, viable community. Focal strain support community. MiSoSoup run anchored focal strain – species solver instructed keep alive exploring viable communities around . species solution support: MILP picks focal strain achieves requested objective. single focal-strain / substrate combination typically yields many alternative optima distinct support compositions; become separate list elements share substrate / strain prefix. Biological scope. 56 solutions span three focal strains (A1R12, A3R04, B3M02) three carbon sources: acetate (ac), citrate (cit), fructose-6-phosphate (f6p). growth medium minimal medium named substrate sole carbon source (see source paper full medium composition). Naming convention. List element names follow pattern {substrate}_{focal-strain}_{solution-id}. example, ac_A1R12_1 alternative optimum 1 focal strain A1R12 acetate. substrate / strain grouping can recovered splitting names underscores. Caution – alternative optima. Two solutions substrate / strain prefix alternative optima MILP, biologically independent communities. pairwise FOS overlap expected high construction; treat overlaps solver-consistency diagnostics rather ecological signal. Relationship importMisosoup(). raw MiSoSoup YAML output, use importMisosoup(), parses nested format attaches per-solution biomass medium metadata resulting CMs. misosoup24 list already flattened long form ConsortiumMetabolism() accepts directly, medium composition implicit rather stored element. Metabolite identifiers. BiGG (Biochemically, Genetically Genomically structured) knowledge base identifiers (https://bigg.ucsd.edu; King et al. 2016). Raw exchange reaction IDs flux-balance simulators (e.g. R_EX_ac_e, EX_ac_e) normalised bare metabolite names importMisosoup() via normalize_ids = TRUE (default).

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
text: Retrieves pathways representing metabolic interactions species. default, returns concise summary columns consumed, produced, n_species. ConsortiumMetabolismSet objects, n_cons included well. Set verbose = TRUE return full pathway data including flux statistics, indices, per-species detail. argument type can used return specific types pathways ConsortiumMetabolismSet object: "" returns pathways "pan-cons" returns pathways present consortia "niche" returns niche pathways specific consortia "core" returns core metabolic pathways shared across species "aux" returns auxiliary pathways found species ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment objects, type selects pathway subset: "" returns union pathways "shared" (pairwise ) returns pathways shared query reference "unique" (pairwise ) returns pathways unique query reference list "consensus" (multiple ) returns consensus network pathways prevalence

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
text: object ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object. ... Object specific arguments. See methods details. verbose Logical scalar. FALSE (default), returns concise summary columns consumed, produced, n_species. TRUE, returns full pathway data including flux statistics, effective values, per-species detail. type Character scalar giving type pathways output. Note four filtered types use two different rulers: "pan-cons" "niche" rank integer per-pathway consortium count quantile uniform integer ruler (seq_len(total_cons)); "core" "aux" rank per-pathway species count quantile empirical n_species distribution across pathways. quantileCutoff Numeric scalar 0 1 giving quantile threshold use filtering pathways. "pan-cons" "core" types, pathways strictly 1 - quantileCutoff returned. "niche" "aux" types, pathways quantileCutoff returned (boundary included lower tail ties quantile floor silently dropped). Defaults 0.1 (.e., top/bottom 10 percent).

title: Plot a ConsortiumMetabolism object — plot,ConsortiumMetabolism,ANY-method
text: x ConsortiumMetabolism object. type Character. eight CM assays render. One : "Binary" Presence / absence pathway (consumed \(\rightarrow\) produced). "nSpecies" Count species participate pathway. "Consumption" Total consumption flux per pathway (sum uptake fluxes). "Production" Total production flux per pathway (sum secretion fluxes). "EffectiveConsumption" Consumption flux scaled Hill-1 perplexity, \(F \cdot 2^{H(p)}\); units Consumption combines magnitude species evenness across pathway. "EffectiveProduction" production side. "nEffectiveSpeciesConsumption" Hill-1 effective number consuming species. Unitless, \([1, S]\). "nEffectiveSpeciesProduction" production side.

title: Plot a Directed Graph Emphasising Flow — plotDirectedFlow
text: Arranges nodes directed graph three distinct columns representing sources (nodes outgoing edges), sinks (nodes incoming edges), intermediate nodes (incoming outgoing edges). layout within intermediate column determined using Kamada-Kawai algorithm. visualisation helps understand overall flow structure within graph rendered ggraph, returned object composes usual ggplot2 operators.

Used edgeColourAttr non-NULL. edgeColourLabels Optional named character vector mapping levels edgeColourAttr legend labels. Used edgeColourAttr non-NULL. edgeColourLegendTitle Character scalar. Legend title categorical edge colour scale. Defaults "Type". edgeColourLow Character string. colour lowest edge weight colourEdgesByWeight TRUE. title: ramen (development version)
text: EffectiveConsumption EffectiveProduction assays now store flux-corrected effective flux F⋅2H(p)F \cdot 2^{H(p)} (matching equation 2 underlying thesis), bare Hill-1 perplexity 2H(p)2^{H(p)}. units Consumption / Production assays. Pre-Bioconductor release, users yet, deprecation cycle. Two new assays expose previous quantity explicit names: nEffectiveSpeciesConsumption nEffectiveSpeciesProduction carry Hill-1 effective number contributing species (unitless, ∈[1,S]\[1, S]), mirroring existing nSpecies count. Algebraic identity: EffectiveConsumption = Consumption * nEffectiveSpeciesConsumption (modulo two-decimal rounding). consortia() longer ConsortiumMetabolism method. plural noun applies containers consortia, accessor now scoped ConsortiumMetabolismSet (returns list constituent CMs). underlying edge list single ConsortiumMetabolism, use .data.frame(cm). consortia(cma) continues raise error – design, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment result object records inputs’ names rather retaining copies . plotDirectedFlow() parameters renamed lowerCamelCase throughout, British-English spelling colour-related args. size-bearing arguments (nodeSize, nodeLabelSize, edgeArrowSize) now use ggraph millimetre units rather igraph cex factors, defaults adjusted legibility. hard rename – aliases old names.

title: ramen (development version)
text: .data.frame() now defined ConsortiumMetabolism (returns edge list columns met, species, flux) ConsortiumMetabolismSet (row-binds per-CM edges prefixes consortium column). pre-existing ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment method unchanged.

title: ramen (development version)
text: importMisosoup() gains biomassPattern argument (Perl regex) users can point arbitrary biomass-reaction names. default matches Growth_<sp>, R_Biomass_<sp>, R_R_BIOMASS_<sp> case-insensitively (MiSoSoup’s biomass reaction name model-dependent). importMisosoup() now records CMSC/MSC provenance returned CM metadata(cm)$misosoupMode ("CMSC" "MSC") metadata(cm)$focalStrain (character NA). community_growth summary row captured metadata(cm)$communityGrowth instead leaking media bucket. Internal documentation, comments, user-facing CLI messages standardised British English spelling (colour, centre, organise, etc.). External-API identifiers upstream packages (igraph, ggplot2, ColorBrewer) keep original spelling. Language: en-GB DESCRIPTION field follow separate commit.

title: ramen 0.0.0.9001
text: Initial development release. Three core S4 classes: ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment, inheriting TreeSummarizedExperiment. Import microbial metabolic network data MiSoSoup YAML format via importMisosoup(). Pairwise multiple alignment consortium metabolisms using functional overlap scores (FOS), Jaccard, Bray-Curtis, redundancy overlap metrics. Visualisation methods including heatmaps, network plots, alignment score plots. Functional group analysis hierarchical clustering species shared metabolic pathways.
